records.title: Welcome to the CSO Knowledge Centre
records.description: A warm welcome to the CSO Knowledge Centre, a living library for surrogacy, egg donation, family building, and the knowledge CSO has gathered over more than 30 years.
records.author: Robyn Price
records.internalLinksNeeded: My Story By Joanne Wright; Program Guide; What is surrogacy in Canada?; Who can be a surrogate?
records.content:
Welcome to the CSO Knowledge Centre.

This is more than a collection of articles. It is the beginning of a living library built from more than 30 years of real experience in Canadian surrogacy, egg donation, family building, matching, loss, hope, advocacy, and love.

Some of what you find here will answer practical questions. How does surrogacy work in Canada? What does it cost? Who can become a surrogate? What should intended parents know before they begin?

Some of it will go deeper. Into the stories, the hard lessons, the changing laws, the medical realities, the relationships, and the quiet moments that shape this work long before a baby is placed into someone's arms.

Canadian Surrogacy Options was built on the belief that people deserve clear information, honest guidance, and compassionate support. That belief started with Joanne Wright, Canada's first surrogate and the founder of CSO, and it continues today as we rebuild, preserve, and share the knowledge gathered across decades of helping families come together.

Our goal is simple: to make surrogacy and egg donation easier to understand, easier to navigate, and less lonely for everyone involved.

Whether you are an intended parent, a surrogate, an egg donor, a professional, a journalist, or someone just beginning to learn what this world is all about, you are welcome here.

Follow along as we grow this library, recover our history, answer the questions people are afraid to ask, and work toward transforming the surrogacy and egg donation world through knowledge, transparency, and care.
records.whyNow: Sets the tone for the Knowledge Centre before the first historical article goes live.
records.whyLater: Can be published after Robyn review as the welcome piece for the library.
records.whyNot: No hard blocker; keep draft until Robyn approves.

records.title: My Story By Joanne Wright
records.description: Why I chose to be a Gestational CarrierвҖҰ orвҖҰ YESвҖҰ Surrogacy in Canada Happens all the Time!!!! Having completed our family, and knowing the joy that children can bring, it seemed so sad, that many couples, through no fau
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Why I chose to be a Gestational CarrierвҖҰ orвҖҰ

YESвҖҰ Surrogacy in Canada Happens all the Time!!!!

Having completed our family, and knowing the joy that children can bring, it seemed so sad, that many couples, through no fault of their own were unable to experience this fulfillment. Technology has come a long way. Couples can now have a biological child through a third party.

I married my wonderful husband Roland in 1979, and we will be celebrating our 29th anniversary this year. We also have three great children. Robyn (1982), Adam (1984), and Kevin (1987). And we added a new member of our family this year. Our first grandchild was born. Cameron Ashton Roland is currently the light of our livesвҖҰ

Even before we had children, I had heard the word вҖңsurrogateвҖқ and knew that this was something I could doвҖҰ..but filed it away for future reference.

After our family was complete, I went on to look at this more seriously.

My only option at that time was to travel to the United States and meet with an agency. Within two months they called and had a couple for me in Ontario. I was thrilled, excited and scared.

We met in July 1987 and hit it off right away. They were a fabulous couple who already had a child, but for medical reasons were advised not to attempt any more pregnancies.

We all, for the most part understood the вҖңlogisticsвҖқ of surrogacy, but not the enormity of the task that we would be taking on, or the ground breaking that we would have to do.

We also decided to work with an IVF clinic in Michigan, who were fabulous, and very accommodating. I was going to be a gestational surrogate. I would have no biological link to the baby. They would be using the Intended MotherвҖҷs egg, and the Intended FatherвҖҷs sperm.

We all had to go through psychological testing as well as find lawyers. I have to tell you, there were not any lawyers and counsellors jumping up and down for our business 21 years ago. I called on my family lawyer, who was our real estate lawyer. When I look back, he probably thought I was on crack when I told him what I wanted to undertake. But he agreed, nonetheless, and the Intended Parents also located a wonderful lawyer willing to take on this monumental task.

Finally, we cross our TвҖҷs and dot our IвҖҷs, and it is time for the retrieval and transfer. We decided to transfer 4 embryos. Now the wait begins. After a very long 10 days, I pee on a stick, and it is positive right away. I called the couple and woke them up, screaming, вҖҰвҖқIвҖҷm PregnantвҖқвҖҰ.. I am sure they thought there was a mad woman on the other end, but they were thrilled.

Our first ultrasound showed 3 possibly four sacs. AAHHHHHHвҖҰ..This is probably the first time I had ever considered multiples. I was in shock at the thought of carrying four babies.

God works in mysterious ways, and our next ultrasound showed that the embryos has naturally reduced to twins. We all breathed a huge sigh of relief.

After a fairly uneventful pregnancy, their son and daughter were born. I cannot even begin to describe the feelings I had. I had such a wonderful sense of accomplishment and pride. I never at any time felt that these babies were any part of me. They belonged to their parents. This couple now had their family. We talked intermittently after the birth, but ultimately they moved and we lost touch with each other, which was ok. Most surrogate arrangements tend to have a вҖңChristmas CardвҖқ relationship, where they keep in touch once or twice a year.

I never really had any intentions of doing this again, until three years later, a friend introduced me to a couple, that also had a child, but again, for medical reasons couldnвҖҷt attempt another pregnancy. I thought I would just help them with information etc, but after I heard their story, and how desperately they wanted another child, how could I deny them?

We had a fabulous relationship, and I think because I had done this before they didnвҖҷt have any fears that couples typically do during this processвҖҰthe most important beingвҖҰвҖқWill she give us our babyвҖқвҖҰ

We again transferred four embryos. I did a Home pregnancy test on MotherвҖҷs Day, and it was POSITIVE! At our first ultrasound I was disappointed to learn that I was only carrying one baby. Almost like I didnвҖҷt do a good enough job. The couple, of course was thrilled. And even more so, when their son was born. We had a wonderful birth, with a very supportive hospital staff. But I still had the feeling of вҖңmy best friend just had a new babyвҖқ. No attachment feelings at allвҖҰI was only the вҖңbabysitterвҖқ. I was вҖңsadвҖқ, when all was said and done, but nothing out of the ordinary.

After I completed my second surrogacy, I got involved with a number of professionals involved in surrogacy. Lawyers, clinic personnel, counsellors etc., all trying to put some regulation into surrogacy.

During this time, we found quite surprisingly that people were getting the run-around. Yes, they needed a surrogate but clinics were not able to help match. As well, potential surrogates would call, but no one quite knew what to do with them.

At that time, I was a stay at home mom, so I offered to be the вҖңcontactвҖқ person that everyone could call. I answered questions, provided support, and put couples in touch with possible surrogates. And the rest, as they say is history.

For this reason, I decided to take my passion a step further, and created

Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc.

, which is a liaison service as well as providing education for couples needing a surrogate carrier, and for women contemplating becoming a surrogate.

I absolutely love what I have been doing for the past 18 years. It has been a been daunting at times, but it certainly has been challenging. Unfortunately there probably isnвҖҷt a story that I havenвҖҷt heard, and it still breaks my heart every time I do hear someoneвҖҷs personal struggle with infertility.

Surrogate parenting is a wonderful family construction option for many families. It is my hope that people can realize that their dream of having children can be accomplished with a positive surrogacy experience.

I hope that I have helped people along the way with personal experience and a common sense approach to fulfill their quest to вҖҰвҖқMake a coupleвҖҰ a FamilyвҖҰвҖқ

PRESS RELEASE: Canadian Surrogacy Options, CanadaвҖҷs First Surrogacy Company, Celebrates 25 Years Helping Couples Nurture Their Dream Of Having A Family
records.content:
For Immediate Release:

Canadian Surrogacy Options, CanadaвҖҷs First Surrogacy Company, Celebrates 25 Years Helping Couples Nurture Their Dream Of Having A Family

Toronto, ON (September 6, 2016)

вҖ“ Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. (CSO), celebrates its 25

th

year in operations with a planned rebranding rollout in 2017. CSO has hired local boutique advertising and PR company, Solara Communications, to execute the rebrand.

вҖңWe are excited to be a part of Canadian Surrogacy Options story as it goes through its rebranding,вҖқ says Vijanti Ramlogan, President of Solara. вҖңAs CanadaвҖҷs first surrogacy company and twenty-five years working closely with surrogates across Canada to give the gift of a child to couples, CSO has brought together over 2,000 families. Of course, we feel special to be sharing in that history being made. We want CSOвҖҷs rebrand to be reflective of that reputation, the history, the family connection, the knowledge and the impact it has had on the surrogacy field in Canada.вҖқ Ramlogan has over 15 years marketing experience and has worked with many small and large corporations including award-winning rebranding campaigns for some of the Greater Toronto AreaвҖҷs biggest shopping centres.

Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. started in 1992 by Joanne Wright out of Guelph, Ontario and was CanadaвҖҷs first surrogate in 1988. She is a 4-time Gestational Surrogate carrier herself. After the 2

nd

pregnancy, Wright discovered the lack of knowledge and regulation about surrogacy amongst the professional agencies, couples in search of a surrogate and the potential carriers. вҖңIt still breaks my heart every time I do hear someoneвҖҷs personal struggle with infertility,вҖқ says Wright. вҖңRealizing the logistics of surrogacy was the inhibitor to these individuals and agencies, I decided that I wanted to help simplify the process so no couple can be deprived of the joy and life-changing experience that it is to hold a biological baby in their arms. WrightвҖҷs daughter, Robyn Price, Managing Partner of CSO continues assisting couples and surrogates to connect. вҖңThe journey to parenthood can be a difficult one for some couples. WeвҖҷve heard many heartbreaking stories over the decades,вҖқ says Price.

One common thread she hears is the despair that couples face but the light that shines on that journey is when a strong, selfless woman comes along, and whole-heartedly decides to give the gift of a child to complete strangers. Price shares, вҖңthat is the human spirit that keeps Canadian Surrogacy Options in search of these surrogate carriers.вҖқ CSO has planned several events across Canada for the rest of 2016 to spread surrogacy information and to speak directly with anyone interested in becoming a carrier, including the Calgary Baby & Tot Show and the Toronto BabyTime Show in November.

About Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc.:

Established in 1992 by Joanne Wright and now managed by Robyn Price, CSO has 25 years experience helping couples through their infertility issues in search of Canadian surrogates. For more information on Surrogacy and the Upcoming Events, visit

www.canadiansurrogacyoptions.com

or email

admin@canadiansurrogacyoptions.com

and connect with them on their facebook page @CanadianSurrogacyOptionsInc.

About Solara Communications:

Owner and President, Vijanti Ramlogan started Solara in 2009, a full service marketing and advertising company, focused on bringing top-level marketing strategies and superior service to small businesses. Ensuring budgets, objectives and creativity are aligned, Solara helps local GTA clients in the public and not-for-profit sectors realize their growth potential. For more information, visit www.solaracommunications.com.

-30 вҖ“

Media Contacts:

Robyn Price, Managing Partner, Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc.

T: 519-220-0555

E: robyn@canadiansurrogacyoptions.com

Vijanti Ramlogan, President, Solara Communications

T: 647-831-5400

E:

vijanti@solaracommunications.com

Photo Credits:

Caption

Robyn Price (centre), Managing Partner at Canadian Surrogacy Options, meets with Surrogates (left, Jenna Spencer; right, Christina McCarville) for consultation at ReproMed, a Toronto Fertility Clinic with over 15 yearsвҖҷ experience.

Left to Right

Jenna Spencer

вҖ“ Lives in Trenton, Ontario; 2-time Surrogate; 3 children of her own

Robyn Price

вҖ“ Managing Partner, Canadian Surrogacy Options

Christina McCarville

вҖ“ Lives in Wellington Station, Prince Edward Island; 5-time Surrogate; 2 children of her own

records.title: Creating Families Through Surrogacy With Canadian
records.description: Creating families through surrogacy with Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. OUR MISSION Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. CSO Inc. has a mission to create a balance between supporting the Surrogate, and the recipients who nee
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
tags: Creating Families Through Surrogacy With Canadian
keywords: creating families through surrogacy with canadian
Creating Families Through Surrogacy With Canadian
records.content:
Creating families through surrogacy

with

Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc.

OUR MISSION

Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. (CSO Inc.) has a mission to create a balance between supporting the Surrogate, and the recipients who need one through this wonderful, yet highly emotional journey.

We provide complete support and guidance throughout the surrogate process, from medical and psychological screenings, to providing an abundance of information, resources, and assistance every step of the way.

Our assistance is based on you and your needs and desires. Our goal is to see you through an amazing adventure in surrogacy.

WHO WE ARE:

Joanne, founder and president of Canadian Surrogacy Options (CSO Inc) has more than 25 years experience in the field of surrogacy, both personal and professional. She was a Gestational Surrogate four times, egg donor three times, and over the years has made it her lifeвҖҷs goal to continue to help couples become families. Joanne has a solid reputation among many professionals, parents and surrogates with whom she has worked with.

Joanne has built a solid team within CSO Inc. consisting of Accountants, Administrative Assistants, Surrogate Support Personal, and Technical Staff. She understands the importance of prompt, in depth, and solid service for all CSO clients, and continues to be hands on with the CSO team.

REQUIREMENTS

The Recipients that CSO helps must be physically unable to carry a child.

The women who will help a Recipient with the support of CSO must be a mother between the ages of 20 and 45, financially self supportive, and overall healthy and able to carry a pregnancy.

COORDINATION

Your choice to work with CSO Inc. provides you with a guide, teacher, and advocate through the maze of information you will encounter during your pursuit of a successful parenting relationship.

CSO Inc offers complete coordination for your journey from identifying and screening your potential surrogate/Intended Parents, right up to the legalities after baby is born. Services included but not limited to are;

Complimentary consultation

Identification, Screening and Coordination for Recipient(s) and Surrogate Mothers

Locate separate legal counsel for both Surrogates and Recipient(s)

Information and guidance on what to expect during the journey

Coordination of all travel requirements (flight, hotel, transportation) for Surrogate Mothers

Coordination of medical appts,

Assist with obtaining legal information pre to post journey

Assist with Life Insurance for Surrogates

Overseeing financial matters between recipients and surrogates

Attend IVF/Embryo Transfer if requested

Attend delivery if requested (within driving distance)

Private Support/Connection groups for both surrogates and recipients

Counseling and mediation (as needed)

SAME-SEX, SINGLE and INTERNATIONAL PARENTS

CSO is highly experienced with International Couples, Same-sex, and single parent families, and understands the unique and diverse needs of various families and situations. From communication, emotional, medical and legal needs, CSO has helped many navigate through these extraordinary needs.

records.title: CSO Family Positive Rituals & Good Luck Charms
records.description: CSO Family Positive Rituals & Good Luck Charms Our Founder, Joanne, believed in positive thinking and having hope every step of the Surrogacy Journey. Our SurroSisters and CSO Family have always empowered each other with
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
tags: CSO Family Positive Rituals & Good Luck Charms
keywords: CSO Family Positive Rituals & Good Luck Charms
records.content:
CSO Family Positive Rituals

& Good Luck Charms

Our Founder, Joanne, believed in positive thinking and having hope every step of the Surrogacy Journey. Our SurroSisters and CSO Family have always empowered each other with good vibes and caring thoughts. WeвҖҷve also seen that creating fun rituals and having lucky charms also help bring a little extra positivity and good luck! Here are a few that are still used often that weвҖҷve come up with over the years!

The orange cup originated from a surrogate get together years ago, when a SurroSister suggested taking home their left-over orange solo cups to use for their pregnancy tests. At the time, it was meant to be a silly joke. However, in a twist of fate, every woman who took a cup home that night later ended up with positive pregnancy tests! Word spread quickly, and soon everyone wanted to have one of these вҖңspecialвҖқ orange cups.

As luck would have it, the streak continued and to this day we still consider it a surrogacy tradition as the colour orange also symbolizes optimizations, energy and good health.

Lucky transfer socks first started in the IVF community for those who were having a difficult time conceiving the natural way. It is said when you wear these socks during an embryo transfer, they bring you extra good luck and sticky dust!

When CSO Family started coming together, it wasnвҖҷt long before our surrogates also adopted this tradition and started wearing lucky socks on Transfer Day. Wearing these socks are not only a fun good luck charm, but also bring an exciting positive energy to the clinic and their IPвҖҷs!

Joanne, our Founder believed that making a stop for French Fries (and sometimes a Milkshake) following a Transfer would ensure the little Embryo would snuggle up for the long haul!

Since JoanneвҖҷs passing, it has not only become a surrogacy ritual, but also a gesture to honour to her; CSO FamilyвҖҷs Mama Bear.

Science has shown us that pineapples contain Bromelain, a blood thinner, anticoagulant, and anti-inflammatory agent which all play a part in helping the embryo implant into the uterus. The core contains a lot of these nutrients, so itвҖҷs important not to cut that part out.

Traditionally itвҖҷs eaten in fifths starting the day of Transfer. WeвҖҷve seen surrogates portion it up, juice it, and freeze puree into ice cubes or popsicles!

Sending вҖңSticky DustвҖқ to a SurroSister on Transfer Day or during the 2 week wait is a phrase that means вҖңWe hope the little embryo sticks and good luck!вҖқ

Since the uterine lining needs to be thick and вҖҳfluffyвҖҷ for the embryo transfer, during a surrogateвҖҷs lining check at the fertility clinic, sending вҖңFluffy ThoughtsвҖқ is another way we say, вҖңGood Luck!вҖқ and send positive vibes for hope and success!

Surrogacy is legal in Canada. However, Surrogacy Laws in Canada have some restrictions.

In Canada, itвҖҷs illegal to pay for the services of a Surrogate or to purchase human sperm and eggs. Only altruistic (unpaid) Surrogacy is allowed. All of our Surrogates carry for their Intended Parents out of the goodness of their hearts.

Surrogates must also be 21 years of age or older.

Commercialized Surrogacy Agencies are also illegal. Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. is not a commercialized Surrogacy Agency.

For full details, visit this link to find CanadaвҖҷs

Assisted Human Reproduction Act.

Reach out to us if you have any further questions or concerns. We are more than happy to help!

What to Expect at your Surrogacy Screening Appointment!
records.content:
Once a connection is made with your Intended Parents, the next step will be your Screening appointment with a Reproductive Clinic. Here is a detailed breakdown of what to expect during the screening process.

First you will be asked to complete a Medical Questionnaire and bring it with you the day of your screening. It will be reviewed by one of the Registered Nurses at the clinic during your appointment. This questionnaire will cover general information such as:

вҖ“ Pregnancy history

вҖ“ Complete medical profile

вҖ“ Medical history of your immediate family

вҖ“ Personal profile of expectations, general thoughts, and preferences for surrogacy

The Nurse will also complete a Physical Examination to determine general overall health. Examples include:

вҖ“ Checking vital signs

вҖ“ Physical examination for potential conditions

They will also take a Blood and Cervical Culture Samples. They send these samples to a Laboratory Technician to run tests for things such as HIV, hepatitis A, B & C, VDRL, CMV, rubella immunity, various hormones, etc.

A Sonohysterogram will also be performed. This is an ultrasound using a saline solution injection to make images of the uterine cavity, cervix, fallopian tubes and ovaries. You may want to take Ibuprofen or Acetaminophen 30 minutes prior to your Sonohysterogram as it may cause period like cramping. Light spotting may also occur for some.

Lastly, a Personal Interview will be arranged with a social worker or psychologist. This is done to make sure you understand the emotional aspect of surrogacy, have a support system in place, and that you and your Intended Parents are on the same page when it comes to expectations.

After completion of these tests, you will meet with the Doctor to discuss your protocol and general time frame for cycle syncing with your Intended Mother or Egg Donor.

records.title: Meet the Team Tuesday!
records.description: We here at CSOFamily want you to know us.. just as you would your own family! So, We are bringing to you: Meet the Team Tuesday! It only seems fitting that I go first so with that: HI! IвҖҷm Robyn! I am currently the Manag
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
We here at #CSOFamily want you to know us.. just as you would your own family!

So, We are bringing to you: Meet the Team Tuesday!

It only seems fitting that I go first so with that:

HI! IвҖҷm Robyn! I am currently the Managing Partner here at Canadian Surrogacy Options and Little Miracles вҖ“ together known as our CSOFamily!

I have been in the Surrogacy World since I was 4вҖҰ Yup, a whopping 33 years! To say Surrogacy, Egg Donation and Alternative Family building is in my blood would be an understatement. It is my entire life! I live it and breath it daily вҖ“ helping couples navigate this journey; helping women feel empowered by what their body can do and give is the very center of my core being.

For those that maybe donвҖҷt know, my mom Joanne was the very first surrogate in Canada вҖ“ which led to many funny and likely awkward conversations in school! Alas, those are stories for another day рҹҳү

For me, growing up, Surrogacy has always been вҖҳnormalвҖҷ. There was never a stigma to it вҖ“ it just вҖҳwasвҖҷ. Into my teenage and early adult years, I actually took a different career path than my Mom. It wasnвҖҷt until my husband and I had a health scare, that led to a fertility scare and surgery in my early 20вҖҷs that really brought me face-to-face with the reality of what my Mom actually did in her daily lifeвҖҰ We had a doctor say to us (when I was 22 years oldвҖҰ) вҖңIf you were planning on having kids, IвҖҷd urge you to start trying nowвҖқ.

We welcomed our first born, a son, about 18 months laterвҖҰ We were grateful, we were thrilled вҖ“ we knew we had to try to help others as much as we could!

Through her professional network of amazing contacts, I became an Egg Donor..! With my husband by my side, my mom helping with every cycle and my dad to help give injections, it was just so natural to go through and help these other couples!

3 of these cycles were anonymous and I can only hope that they are living their best life!

I knew I didnвҖҷt want to go back to my career life after seeing what we had and what we gone through.. One night, after many talks, My Mom had the bestest idea for us to open Little Miracles. With her guiding hand, experience and gentle nudge of advocacy, Little Miracles was born! Knowing we could make an actual difference in Canada for couples and women wanting to become Egg Donors, we became the first Donor Agency in 2007. As she paved the way for all for Surrogacy in Canada, together we blazed through to support everyone and anyone with Both Surrogacy and Egg Donation. An unstoppable team!

I could write forever about our history, our working lives and our relationship but we may need way more space than what we have here!

In 2013, we learned horrible news .. My Mom was diagnosed with a lung disease. The only cure? A lung transplant. We rallied, we remained positive, and we got to work on a temporary transition to allow her to focus on her health and eventual recovery. She was patient with me, she took her time with planting seeds into my mind to ensure my mind would work as hers did.

Her health took a turn downwards fairly quickly in 2016. She had more bad days than good days and was listed for a transplant. We received this amazing gift in December 2016.

Long story short: The next 3 years were a blurвҖҰ More on that full story to come!

Sadly, we lost our Matriach December 20, 2019. My mom passed away peacefully with us, her family, holding her hand and by her side. That day, I not only lost my вҖҳbusinessвҖҷ partner, but I lost my best friend.

Our motto has always been Onwards and Upwards. I know she wants us to continue to do great things, to help as many as we possibly can and to continue to Support one another.

Over the last 4 years, IвҖҷve had such a fire burning inside me вҖ“ to do more, to do better and to help. This fire has only intensified in the last 3 months, knowing she is still guiding me and leading me forward.

Maybe this was a bit long for a вҖңMeet the Team TuesdayвҖқ.. But at CSOFamily, I want you to feel as though you are a part of my family. And, to me, the only way for that to start is if you know the whole me too.

As for knowing more about me personally, I live in Cambridge, Ontario вҖ“ with my husband of almost 15 years and our 2 beautiful children (Cameron who is 14 now and Erica who is 10!). We also have our FurPup Trace; 2 Ninja Cats Molly and Mason; 2 Fishies and our wee baby bird Apollo!

We like to travel when we can and when we canвҖҷt, we head to PortaBackyarda вҖ“ our own private oasis in our backyard! We are a pretty low-key family that just enjoys the simple life of вҖҳhomeвҖҷ and togetherness!

It is always hard to find words in which to use to describe oneselfвҖҰ I will leave you with quotes from those that know me best and I hope my true character will shine through in what they say!

Be the change that you wish to see in the world.вҖқ вҖ• Mahatma Gandhi

My boss, colleague and dear friend- your passion to make a difference in the world is unbelievable. You put your heart and soul into your endeavors and surpass any personal goal you could have set. Watching you do what you love and watching you make a difference in the lives of others is an incredible experience. The love you have for your family is outstanding. Watching you interact with loved ones just makes me smile. You inspire me to be the best version of myself. I look up to you. I feel that more people should strive to have the strength and passion that you exemplify in everyday life.

You have taught me so many things and I want to thank you. Thank you for inspiring me in life. Thank you for making me want to be a better person. Thank you Robyn for allowing my to apart of this amazing organization and family.

Robyn is always there for you no matter what. She tries to connect with everyone on a personal level so they arenвҖҷt just a number. The whole CSO family is always there for you in good times and bad!

When I first joined the CSOfamily team I had never had a boss before. I had always worked for myself. Robyn trusted me, and allowed me creative freedom, for me that was so important. IвҖҷm grateful every day for that trust and to get to be a part of this amazing family

Robyn u are such a caring and amazing person вҖҰyou have definitely followed in your motherвҖҷs footsteps ..momma bear would be proud вҖҰ The cso team is beyond amazing вҖҰsupportive at every turn вҖҰa real support system вҖҰ

And I love love being back at cso вҖҰnothing compares to Canadian surrogacy options thatвҖҷs for sure вҖҰI love the support from the team members вҖҰI love the love from all the girls вҖҰeveryone is supported no matter the problem ..even if itвҖҷs not Surrogacy related вҖҰwe truly are a family вҖҰ

When I first considered becoming a surrogate I knew I wanted to work with an agency that was both ethical and personable. Thankfully my research brought me to CSO where you are not left feeling like a number. Robyn takes the time to get to know you and has your best interest at heart. She is always available if you need her and will do her best to answer any questions you might have. CSO is a close knit family and leaves you feeling valued.

I have always loved the вҖңpick your personвҖқ philosophy and that CSO staff seem very comfortable with a surrogateвҖҷs вҖңpersonвҖқ changing. CSO is just an amazing group of dedicated and caring people. I still think you all had my back :). CSO staff will always respond, reach out and let you know they value you as a person, not just as a surrogate. I have never felt like a number here and never been pressured to have a second journey. I have felt so welcomed into a community that (many outside of CSO would say) I do not belong to because I am not a mother.

And I would say the CSO staff had to work extra hard with me because I had never experienced pregnancy before LOLвҖҰso I was double trouble.

CSO is not just an вҖңagency I am withвҖқ it truly is the CSO Family. You are not simply a group of employees, CSO Family is the вҖңcommunityвҖқ of surrogates, surrogate support and staff that together have created an extended family who are always available.

I came to CSO almost a year ago now, and its been such a great experience for me! The team here is small but gosh are the mighty! IвҖҷve learned so much in this last year and I am so thankful to Robyn for giving me the chance to work along side her and the rest of the team! Robyn has always made me feel comfortable to go to her with and questions or concerns I may have and has always been there to help teach me about surrogacy and the amazing experience it is! The dynamic at CSO Family is really that, we are a family! All of us communicate day in and day out and that communication and team work is really what I think makes are surrogates, egg donors, and intended parents feel comfortable to go on this journey with us! CSO is family and I am so grateful to be apart of it!

Robyn, thanks for always being available for questions. Not matter the time or of you are on vacation or not lol you are always there for us. All of the CSO team is always there and if they arenвҖҷt someone from this sisterhood always is

Even though I am geographically far away from the CSO staff, I have always felt a sense of closeness with the care and love thatвҖҷs provided.

I love CSO. I have made so many life long friends and family free. It almost feels like a family name more than any last name I have ever had. (And IвҖҷve had 4!)

I will leave this with just one last note: Always feel free to come to me. Email me just to check in or to say hi! PM or Text if thatвҖҷs your best way to communicate. Always know, I am in your corner and I am here to advocate for YOU on your journey вҖ“ whether as becoming an Intended Parent OR becoming a Surrogate. I want you to feel empowered and вҖҳheardвҖҷ for what ever it is that you need to make this journey an unforgettable and enjoyable one!

Love and (virtual!) Hugs,

Robyn

records.title: The Top 5 Reasons To Become A Surrogate
records.description: The decision to become a surrogate is a big one. ItвҖҷs a life altering decision that involves the support of many people in your life вҖ“ but there are so many wonderful positive things associated with it! If you have been
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
tags: The Top 5 Reasons To Become A Surrogate
keywords: The Top 5 Reasons To Become A Surrogate
records.content:
The decision to become a surrogate is a big one. ItвҖҷs a life altering decision that involves the support of many people in your life вҖ“ but there are so many wonderful positive things associated with it! If you have been thinking about becoming a surrogate, you have probably read a lot of information, but perhaps you havenвҖҷt thought about the heart-warming emotional aspects of your decision.

Here are 5 reasons why you should become a surrogate:

You are giving the gift of life to a family

Giving the

gift of life

is an amazing experience. There are many couples who cannot have a child together for several reasons. Being a surrogate is giving these couples something they want very badly but cannot do alone, and you are contributing to something so important and special to another family.

You are helping families in different dynamics who cannot produce

Same sex couples who want to have a family often turn to surrogacy so

they can have a child of their own

. Your commitment to surrogacy for these couples is allowing them to experience the gift of life when they otherwise would not have been able to.

ItвҖҷs a special way to give back

Not everyone can donate money, but they can volunteer their time. Being a surrogate is a very special way for you, or you and your family to

give back to another family

. There are many families who rely on the process of surrogacy to help them complete their family. Without your commitment, their family would be incomplete.

It is an emotionally satisfying experience

The emotional satisfaction you will get from providing a couple with a way to complete their family is incomparable to anything else. You are providing them with a solution to something that is wanted very much. Throughout the process you will experience excitement, love, joy and many other positive feelings with the couple you are being a surrogate for.

ItвҖҷs an amazing emotional journey that will be unforgettable

It is a rewarding experience you can share with another family

There are many different ways you can give back in life, but nothing is more rewarding than being able to provide the gift of life. You have the opportunity to see how important this experience is for a couple, and

how much happiness a child that you helped provide them with brings to their lives.

We understand that becoming a surrogate is a major decision that will impact your life in several ways. But as you process your decision, remember all the amazing emotional aspects tied to the process and experience of being a surrogate. CSO is available to guide you through each step, and we are available to answer questions as you make your decision.

My Story

My Story Part 2

Imagine my surprise 10 years later, when I got the urge to be a surrogate again.

During the course of my business, I had met with a couple that had recently had

a baby.

While enjoying a fairly uneventful pregnancy and delivery, she started to haemorrhage. In order to save her life, a hysterectomy had to be performed. At 29 years old, she was never going to be pregnant again.

When I met them they were still in the information gathering stage, and needing to know that surrogacy was a viable option for them. I assured them that it was.

They called several times over the next few years just to touch base, so that when they called and said that they were ready to begin, we had already gotten to know each other. I offered to be their surrogate and they accepted.

The Intended Mom went through the egg retrieval process in February 2002. 19 eggs were retrieved. 12 fertilized. At the end of the first day, the clinic called to say it didnвҖҷt look good. We were going to do a transfer possibly on day 2 (normally transfer

doesnвҖҷt take place till between day 3 and 5), because there were only 2, maybe three embryos remaining.

We ended up doing a day 3 transfer. But just before the transfer, the doctor wanted to talk with us. He just about fell over himself apologizing for the number of embryos,

and the quality. Apparently they were not very good at all. But he assured us, with the

next retrieval he would do a different protocol and things hopefully would be better.

I couldnвҖҷt believe what I was hearing. Basically we were just вҖңgoing through the motionsвҖқ of a transfer, but not a lot of hope.

Out of the three embryos, one wasnвҖҷt doing very well, so we decided to transfer two embryos and see what happened to the third, but we were told it would probably stop developing and die.

I adhered totally to the doctorвҖҷs orders. No baths, only showers, shots in the butt

every day etcвҖҰ

Just for fun, I did a home pregnancy test on day 9. It was POSITIVE. To say I was surprised was an understatement. I showed

everyone

that stick, just to make sure I wasnвҖҷt seeing things. They all assured me it was positive. Then the вҖңWhat was I thinkingвҖқ hit??? Here I was 42 years old. Could I do this? Well on Day 10, 11, and 12, pee on a sticks convinced me, I was indeed pregnant. I hadnвҖҷt told the couple yet, because I wanted the blood test to confirm it. I had made up a wonderful вҖңbaby basketвҖқ, with all kinds of great baby stuff, and attached the вҖңpee on a sticksвҖқ to it. We patiently waited for Day 12. I had made arrangements to meet up with the Intended Mom, already knowing what the test results would reveal.

I went in early in the morning to the clinic, and told them to ONLY call me when the result came in. They smiled knowingly.

I was in the middle of a meeting when the phone rang. The nurse said, вҖңJoanneвҖҰitвҖҷs positiveвҖқ, and gave me the beta number. Even though I already knew the result, the tears streamed down my face.

When I got off the phone, the woman I was with saidвҖҰвҖқitвҖҷs positive isnвҖҷt it?вҖқ I nodded. She saidвҖҰвҖқJoanne, thatвҖҷs how the story should goвҖҰвҖқ

After I left the meeting, I went to meet the Intended Mother. Carrying the huge box, (baby basket), I sat in front of her. She said вҖңWhat?вҖқ I said, вҖңOpen itвҖқ, she did and pulled out the huge basket. She looked at me with tears in her eyes and said, вҖҰвҖқDid you hear somethingвҖқ? I said..вҖқYes, you guys are gonna have a babyвҖқ, She said NO! I saidвҖҰYES! She immediately got on the phone to the Intended dad, to share our news.

We all couldnвҖҷt believe with the crappy quality of the embryos that it actually worked. Their lone embryo also continued to develop to blastocyst, before freezing.

Our first ultrasound showed one baby. We were all thrilled and truly thankful. Our pregnancy was textbook. Our relationship was even better than I could have ever imagined. The Intended Mom made it to every doctorвҖҷs appointment, while the Intended dad made it most of the time.

We emailed and spoke often. I let them know how I was feeling. The good, the bad, and the milestones throughout the pregnancy. I called when the baby kicked for the first time, when I was tired and had heartburn, and when he would do somersaults.

Both of their families got very involved, which was so wonderful for me. Both sets of parents lived out of the country, but we also emailed and spoke often.

Finally it was time!!! The doctor decided we could start inducing on Friday, November 8th, 2002.

We were all to be at the hospital at 7:30 am. We were all filled with such anticipation.

I couldnвҖҷt believe that they would be holding their son (yes, we found out it was a boy) in less than 24 hours. After a couple of false starts, everything gets going, and we

also get the вҖңGood RoomвҖқ. Which was fabulous, because there would be a lot of

people there.

After a very long day, our doctor who had been fabulous, came to tell us his shift was over, but that he would come back for the delivery. In the meantime, he wanted to introduce us to his вҖңbossвҖқ, the doctor that would be delivering. He saidвҖҰвҖқJoanne, here is Doctor LivingstoneвҖқ. I looked up at him, and said вҖңOh my Gosh, did you used to work at Toronto General?вҖқ He said, вҖңWhy yes, I didвҖқ. My eyes misted up, and I said, вҖңYou delivered my surrogate twinsвҖқ, which he remembered right away. I believe in fate, and this was a BIG sign for me. That this was meant to be. He was there for my first delivery and my last.

Finally at 11:45 pm, I am fully dilated and ready to push. My hubby holds one leg, and the Intended Father, the other. The Intended Mother just couldnвҖҷt watch, and was standing by my head. I push once, and the nurse says, hold up a bit. Goes over to the Intended Mom, and says, вҖңHold out your handвҖқ, with a confused look on her face, she does, and the nurse slaps a glove on her. Even more confused now, the nurse says, вҖңCome over here, you are gonna feel your babyвҖҷs headвҖқ. The look of horror on her face was priceless. But she looked over at me and I saidвҖҰвҖқGo aheadвҖқ. She did, and then completely broke down.

Three more pushes and he was out. The dad cut his cord. The mom and dad oohhed and ahhhed over him, and then they brought him to me. I have to say, he was the most beautiful baby I had ever seen (next to mine of course). His eyes were open and he was looking around, just like he had the most incredible journey.

12:31 am November 9th, 2002 7 lbs 9 oz.

The look of joy and wonder on the new parents faces still brings tears to my eyes.

I cannot imagine a better вҖңend to the storyвҖқ than how I spent that weekend. I hope all of you at one time or another get to feel what I and his parents felt that day. вҖҰThat another miracle was bornвҖҰ

records.title: #MySurrogacyJourney | Quick Check In
records.description: Had my monthly OB appointment yesterday and was a little nervous to talk about weight gain. In all seriousness I am still 2.5lbs under my вҖңpre-pregnancyвҖқ weight, but I was down 14lbs so I am back up 11.5lbs. I just need
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Had my monthly OB appointment yesterday and was a little nervous to talk about weight gain. In all seriousness I am still 2.5lbs under my вҖңpre-pregnancyвҖқ weight, but I was down 14lbs so I am back up 11.5lbs. I just need to remind myself that at this stage with my own kids I was already up 20+ lbs so I am WAY ahead of the game. Doctor said nothing about my weight and seemed quite happyвҖҰ the nurse actually told me to eat more often throughout the day Ha!

I took a вҖңweekly bump photoвҖқ today and cannot believe how big this thing is for being 22 weeks! I am measuring 2.5 weeks ahead of what I am (which is also new to me) but that could have a few different reasonsвҖҰ. a large amount of amniotic fluid is one and I completely agree with that. When I had Rosie the nurses had made a comment that they have never seen so much fluid accompany a birth, made her going head down difficult and it didnвҖҷt actually happen until labour started but all worked out

Sent a message to the parents today. I had a few things on my mind that I felt I needed to know the answer to before my head exploded.

1) How are they planning on feeding this baby? Usually a baby has its вҖңfirst feedвҖқ 30-60 minutes after birth. Will they be giving her a bottle of formula? Are they wanting me to provide milk for then or perhaps the first couple weeks?

2) What are their expectations of the labour and birth itself? Are they planning on being with me through it all? Through just the pushing? Maybe they donвҖҷt want to be there at all and wait until she is actually born? Maybe only the mother wants to be part of the whole process? I will admit, if I ever do this again those are some questions I will be asking before any pregnancy happens.

My IPs are amazing and I really have to expectations nor am I all for, or all against anything so I really need their guidance here. I love birth and the whole process so it really doesnвҖҷt matter to me who is there to experience itвҖҰ. just be ready to feel my wrath during transition

I am a little afraid of the first questionвҖҰ My body is terrible at producing (well keeping) milk, so I would hate to be set up for failure. I am pretty confident I could supply a weeks worth but after that is anyones guess. I also am not sold on the whole pumping every 2 hours thingвҖҰ. especially in the middle of the night. But like I said, a week or so I could do

Phil made the comment the other day of вҖңso, when you are in labour I just stay at home with the kids? Do the parents see you when youвҖҷre in labour or just after the baby is born?вҖқ ok Phil, I get its a little complicated and uncharted territory for all of us but do you honestly think I am labouring and pushing out a baby ON MY OWN?! IвҖҷm pretty sure he thought he was getting out of this one haha. Boy was he wrong

Baby girls movements are much stronger now. She is a relaxed baby, she never gets too crazy in thereвҖҰ. i think she is going to be a wonderful infant

I was actually able to see a large kick last night while watching tv. Now we are getting into the fun part of pregnancy! I canвҖҷt wait for the rest <3

This post was written by our guest surrogate blogger. Follow her journey through #MySurrogacyJourney

What We Offer to Intended Parents

At

Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. (CSO)

, we understand that embarking on a surrogacy journey is a significant and emotional decision. With over 30 years of experience as CanadaвҖҷs first and longest-running surrogacy agency, we are committed to providing comprehensive support tailored to your unique path to parenthood.:contentReference[oaicite:11]{index=11}

Our Comprehensive Services Include:

Personalized Consultation:

A dedicated one-on-one free consultation to address all your questions and concerns.

Profile Development:

Assistance in creating and refining your Intended Parent profile, with unlimited reviews to ensure it reflects your story.

Community Support:

Access to our private Intended Parent Facebook support group, connecting you with others on similar journeys.

Professional Referrals:

Connections to experienced lawyers, clinics, and counselors to guide you through the legal and medical aspects.

Egg Donation Services:

Special referral to our sister company,

Little Miracles Inc.

, for egg donation options.

Surrogate Matching:

Circulation of your profile to available surrogates, facilitating meaningful connections.

Logistical Coordination:

Assistance with booking appointments, travel, and accommodations for both you and your surrogate.

Ongoing Communication:

Regular updates and consistent communication throughout your journey, celebrating milestones together.

Financial Management:

Complete management of reimbursements, including sorting and filing all surrogate receipts and documentation.

Surrogate Support:

Assignment of an individual support worker for your surrogate, ensuring she receives dedicated care.

Relationship Mediation:

Mediation services to foster a positive and supportive connection between you and your surrogate.

In-Person Support:

Availability for in-person support during screening and transfer appointments, as permitted.

Advocacy:

Continuous advocacy for you and your process, ensuring your needs and preferences are prioritized.

Post-Birth Assistance:

Support in securing accommodations for delivery, completing passport documents, and referrals for parentage documents as needed.

International Client Support:

Guidance on entering Canada, including navigating current policies and procedures.

Gestures of Appreciation:

Assistance in sending gifts or flowers to your surrogate, at your discretion.

Our goal is to make your surrogacy journey as smooth and fulfilling as possible, providing unwavering support from your first inquiry to welcoming your child.

records.title: SurroSister Secrets..Part 16!
records.description: SheвҖҷs Back! Our SecretSurro is back with another entry: вҖңDo I have my PIO and needles? Yes, alright. Phone charger, check. Meds? Yes, wait do I have spare PIO?? Spare alcohol swabs. Book for the plane. I CANNOT forget th
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
SheвҖҷs Back! Our SecretSurro is back with another entry:

вҖңDo I have my PIO and needles? Yes, alright. Phone charger, check. Meds? Yes, wait do I have spare PIO?? Spare alcohol swabs. Book for the plane. I CANNOT forget the PIO.вҖқ

For some reason, my brain has translated the pre-transfer jitters into an overwhelming fear IвҖҷm going to forget something important for the trip. Realistically aside from my uterus, thereвҖҷs very few things that I HAVE to bring with me for transfer that I canвҖҷt buy or replace en route. My brain has decided to therefore focus on the medication in my protocol that is most difficult to source, necessary every single day, and that requires many components to administer. My progesterone in oil doesnвҖҷt require remembering just the medication itself, you need the delivery mechanism, the antiseptic swabs, and a plan for sharps disposal. ThereвҖҷs a lot of items I can do without for a quick 48 hour transfer trip, but my PIO isnвҖҷt one of them.

IвҖҷve figured out where this compulsive checking comes from. Basically, instead of my brain consciously fretting about all those thoughts in the back of my head that IвҖҷm trying to ignore (вҖңWhat if transfer fails again? I canвҖҷt stand disappointing my IPs again. What if it takes but then something goes wrong??), my brain is fretting about packing lists and alcohol swabs.

Because truth be told it really is impossible to quiet those thoughts completely. It is possible to simultaneously be choosing hope and optimism, and yet still be battling the shadows of doubt that lurk in the back of your head. My Intended Parents and I are choosing to see all of the things working out and lining up for us this transfer, but of course there are apprehensions too. IвҖҷm thankful there are ways to combat those shadow thoughts, and store up the good ones instead. I say a prayer, and I give thanks for this opportunity, whatever may come. I pick up my phone and I text my Intended Mom about being paranoid IвҖҷm forgetting something.

Then I grab that spare PIO vial and toss it in my bag. Hope. Optimism. Ready.

Thank you SecretSurro!! We canвҖҷt wait to hear how your transfer goes!!!

records.title: Becoming a Surrogate in Canada
records.description: Becoming a surrogate in Canada is a deeply personal and meaningful decision. It is not about attention or recognition. It is about helping a family experience something they may have waited years for. At Canadian Surroga
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Becoming a surrogate in Canada is a deeply personal and meaningful decision. It is not about attention or recognition. It is about helping a family experience something they may have waited years for.

At Canadian Surrogacy Options, we work closely with women who feel a quiet curiosity about surrogacy and want to understand what it truly involves before taking the next step.

What Does It Mean to Be a Surrogate?

A surrogate is a woman who carries a pregnancy for intended parents who are unable to do so on their own. In Canada, surrogacy is altruistic and focused on support, care, and ethical guidance for everyone involved.

Surrogates are supported medically, legally, and emotionally throughout the entire journey. This is not something you do alone.

Who Can Become a Surrogate in Canada?

While every situation is unique, most surrogates meet the following criteria:

Between the ages of 21 and 45

Have had at least one healthy pregnancy

Are physically and emotionally healthy

Live in Canada

Have a stable support system

Our team walks through eligibility carefully and honestly, with no pressure to proceed.

What Support Do Surrogates Receive?

Surrogates with Canadian Surrogacy Options receive guidance every step of the way, including:

Coordination with fertility clinics

Legal referrals and support

Reimbursement for eligible pregnancy related expenses

Emotional support and ongoing communication

We believe surrogates deserve clarity, respect, and care throughout the process.

How to Learn More About Surrogacy

If you have ever felt curious about becoming a surrogate, the best first step is simply learning more. Asking questions does not commit you to anything.

You are welcome to explore surrogacy at your own pace.

рҹ‘ү Learn more or apply here:

https://www.canadiansurrogacyoptions.com

records.title: SurroSister Secrets.. Part 8!
records.description: whirlwind My screening schedule is a whirlwind. Over supper, I travel to the clinic in another city and have a late night insurance meeting. Counselling starts fist thing the next day, then the medical portion of my scre
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
*whirlwind*

My screening schedule is a whirlwind. Over supper, I travel to the clinic in another city and have a late night insurance meeting. Counselling starts fist thing the next day, then the medical portion of my screening starts mid-morning. I am on my way back home by mid-afternoon. Safe to say, IвҖҷm not expecting any down time.

Aside from some nerves at the tight schedule, I have a fair amount of nerves about this screening process itself. In the days leading up to screening IвҖҷve realized that not getting medical clearance would be a pretty obvious standstill to this journey of mine. Now that IвҖҷve pulled the trigger and have my IPs, IвҖҷm basically going to have to be hi-jacked off this path IвҖҷm on. But screening could include a realization that my body isnвҖҷt for some reason compatible, and this could be the end. So there are some nerves.

Nothing actually goes smoothly. In my worry to catch my transit, it slips my mind to pay for parking, and I have to find a panicked online payment method (thank you wifi). I have to change my travel plans last minute when thereвҖҷs a transit issue, and I get to my hotel later than I thought I would for my insurance meeting. I donвҖҷt know that stressed is the best way to go into an early morning counselling sessionвҖҰ..

My chat with the counsellor actually is a great kick off to my morning. I love a good opportunity to hear someoneвҖҷs story, and to tell a bit of mine. SheвҖҷs very kind, and gracious, and itвҖҷs a great start to screening day. ItвҖҷs great to feel heard.

My highlight though comes with my time with the Medical Director. IвҖҷm not sure what I was expecting, but a 20 minute sit down with the director wasnвҖҷt on the list. At least, this level of discussion wasnвҖҷt on my expected list. We talked about my adored intended parents, about my own prenatal history, and about what my protocol might look like. I had some specific requests for medications, as did he, and we went back and forth. He mentioned a recent study he had read, affirming my request for one med above another, but I got a pushback when I requested to miss a different one. I feel like weвҖҷve reached compromise, so I concede. And I canвҖҷt pretend to know the formula that goes into producing this anticipated little one, the actual alchemy of bringing this hoped for babe into the world with a whole lotta love and a whole lotta science. But still, I appreciate feeling heard.

IвҖҷm more tired coming back than I am going, but I have a go-ahead for next steps.

ItвҖҷs getting real.

Signed, SecretSurro

Thank you SecretSurro! These entries sure are so insightful as to your emotions and your вҖҳmindspaceвҖҷ during all of this!!! We love reading them!!

records.title: How to Address Surrogacy With Your Family
records.description: If you are interesting in becoming a surrogate, you likely going to face some very serious conversation and perhaps even some that are intimating. Surrogacy is such a big decision that itвҖҷs important to voice your opinio
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
tags: How to Address Surrogacy With Your Family
keywords: how to address surrogacy with your family
How to Address Surrogacy With Your Family
records.content:
If you are interesting in becoming a surrogate, you likely going to face some very serious conversation and perhaps even some that are intimating. Surrogacy is such a big decision that itвҖҷs important to voice your opinion on your choice, and work with the significant people in your life to decide if surrogacy will be a good fit in your life.

Here are some tips for bringing up the idea of surrogacy to your family and how to handle it:

Do as much research as possible, and be able to show it

You need show what surrogacy is, how it will affect your current situation, the type of surrogacy you want to participate in and what the effects will be on yourself. Explain why you have chosen to do this.

Make sure you listen, and be prepared to hear different points of view

After you have explained your side of the situation, itвҖҷs your turn to listen. Listen respectful and do your best to understand their point of view. They might not have the same opinion as you, and they also might need to time to warm up to the idea. So allow them to get out their own feelings. If it doesnвҖҷt go well the first time, give it a few days to sit.

Try to keep emotions to a minimum

Do your best to not allow your emotions to get the best of the situation. Allowing emotions to overtake the conversation will keep you from getting out everything you want to say and it will change the direction of the conversation as well.

Resist the urge to argue

They may have conflicting opinions about your choice, and you will need to accept this. DonвҖҷt allow the conversation be a reason to get upset with one another. Arguments could decrease your chance for showing a person with conflicting views why surrogacy can be something amazing.

ItвҖҷs important to not be afraid to address a conversation like this. Go into it confidently and without any expectations. You never know how the other person will react. If you would like more information or guidance on how to address surrogacy with your family, feel free to reach out to us!

records.title: Surrogacy Blogs We Love
records.description: We have our own blog because we like to be able to share information and stories with our CSO family. Of course, there are many other blogs out there as well that are related to surrogacy and provide an excellent amount
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
We have our own blog because we like to be able to share information and stories with our CSO family. Of course, there are many other blogs out there as well that are related to surrogacy and provide an excellent amount of literature from helping you with your decision to become a surrogate, intended parent(s) or even provide with helpful information for your journey with surrogacy.

We decided to provide you with a list of surrogacy blogs we think are great. Check them out below:

All Things Surrogacy

This well-rounded surrogacy blog highlights couples, surrogates and agencies to provide you with a huge source of information for first-hand experience with surrogacy. They also feature other surrogate bloggers as well!

Our Misconception

This amazing and honest couple share their experience with infertility and parenthood. ItвҖҷs a great blog to read to learn that you are not alone in this!

Their Bun, My Oven

A blog written first-hand by a surrogate. She openly shares her experience as a surrogate (just like our awesome guest blogger!)

Chrissy & LeeвҖҰOur Surrogacy Journey

Want to hear the intended parentвҖҷs side of the story? Read about their experience and check our their photos and video content!

My Body. Their Baby

This is the story of a 2x surrogate! The blog has not been updated in well over a year, but itвҖҷs still a great read!

We love these blogs because they each tell a story from their own experience. Whether you making a decision, or currently experiencing surrogacy, reading the experience of others can help you relate and gain more insight. You get to know the couple or surrogate and read about their journey. Some of the blogs are a little dated, but still give you aa great read if you start from the beginning.

We try to incorporate as much experience as possible into our own blog, but we also know that there is so much more out there to help you with your own journey. Do you have any other surrogacy blogs you love to read? Tell us in the comments below!

records.title: SurroSister Secrets.. Part 12!!
records.description: Well.. It has been quite some time since weвҖҷve heard from our SecretSurro! SheвҖҷs back with another entry caution, could be difficult for some readers as a trigger . While we always hope for Sunshine and Roses in Surrogac
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Well.. It has been quite some time since weвҖҷve heard from our SecretSurro!

SheвҖҷs back with another entry (caution, could be difficult for some readers as a trigger). While we always hope for Sunshine and Roses in Surrogacy, that is not always the true story.

Dread.

Of all the myriad of feelings and emotions that I thought IвҖҷd experience this journey, this was not one of the ones I had anticipated. I figured IвҖҷd feel some excitement, and some nervousness, a little bit of eagerness, and of course, the surrogateвҖҷs constant companion, impatience. But this unanticipated dread emerged swift and sudden, and it became a daily companion for the whole of my two week wait.

Dreading taking another test and seeing another negative. ThereвҖҷs this moment for some surrogates that intended parents should know happens; sheвҖҷs holding that test in her hand and praying for a line that instinctively she knows isnвҖҷt coming. And sheвҖҷs so heartbroken.

She feels like sheвҖҷs letting them down. That person or couple that sheвҖҷs grown to care about deeply, and that she knows has all their hopes riding on her, she hates with all her heart the disappointment she feels sheвҖҷs causing.

Because we know that sometimes itвҖҷs all their literal eggs in our basket.

Another moment of dread, after fourteen days of waiting, seeing the email with the Beta results you knew would be written, but are some how so much harder to swallow seeing with your own eyes.

вҖңConfirmation of non-pregnancy. Stop all medications.вҖқ

This wasnвҖҷt supposed to happen like this.

Now what do we do?

And with thatвҖҰ We wait with you.. Sending gentle hugs and love to you and your Intended Parents during this next phase вҖ“ and until then, we wait to hear from you again! xoxo

records.title: SurroSister Secrets.. Part 9!
records.description: Thank you SecretSurro!! Looking over a protocol for a gamut of meds about to go into my body. MY body. This body that has been strong enough to bring my sweet babies into the world will be manipulated and medicated and r
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Thank you SecretSurro!!

Looking over a protocol for a gamut of meds about to go into my body.

MY body. This body that has been strong enough to bring my sweet babies into the world will be manipulated and medicated and readied to prepare for a sweet little one. A sweet little that will not be mine, but belongs to another. A sweet babe who is wanted, and wished for, and prayed for, but who needs help getting here.

Deep breaths staring down the protocol IвҖҷve just received. IвҖҷve been checking my email in anticipation of the protocol, and bugging the clinic a mere handful of times. IвҖҷve been so impatient for it to arrive, and now that itвҖҷs here I feel like I need to take a moment to check myself. IвҖҷm learning as I go, and did I anticipate meds? Yes. Did I anticipate a protocol a page long with countless shots and a half a dozen different medications? Yes? Sort of? Somehow seeing it on paper is a different thing than imagining it.

My mind jumps to my intended parents. вҖңI canвҖҷt wait to see them as parents.вҖқ ItвҖҷs the fresh resolve I need to face down the new, and the different, and the intimidating. I get out my calendar and start marking days. Injections, protocol changes, ultrasounds and transfer. This protocol covers a span of almost two months; if a first transfer is successful itвҖҷs up to five months of medications. вҖңA couples forever will change. Their whole life will change, as well as their families lives.вҖқ This is something I can do, this is a good I can do for the world.

A bag of medications, a calendar full of dates, and a heart full of optimism. IвҖҷm ready.

About

Surrogacy FAQs

There are two main types of surrogacy:

Gestational Surrogacy

and

Traditional Surrogacy

. At Canadian Surrogacy Options, weвҖҷre here to help you understand the difference and support you every step of the way.

In gestational surrogacy, the surrogate (also known as the gestational carrier) is not genetically related to the baby. The embryo is created using the egg and sperm of the intended parents or donors, then transferred to the surrogate through IVF.

Most common type of surrogacy in Canada

No genetic link to the surrogate

Offers legal clarity for intended parents

In traditional surrogacy, the surrogate uses her own egg and is genetically related to the child. Insemination is typically done via IUI (intrauterine insemination).

Less common due to legal complexities

Surrogate has a genetic connection to the baby

May not be available in all provinces

How CSO Supports You

Whether youвҖҷre an intended parent exploring your options or a potential surrogate wanting to understand your path, our team is here to guide you:

30+ years of experience in Canadian surrogacy

Legal, emotional, and logistical support

Personalized matches based on shared values and expectations

Advocacy for both gestational and traditional surrogates (when applicable)

records.title: SurroSister Secrets... Part 2!!!!
records.description: Our SecretSurro is back with another entry! We canвҖҷt wait to share with all of you!!!: IвҖҷm the kind of person who requires a posse. When I decided to go back to school and finish my degree, I had hubby who is an educatio
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Our SecretSurro is back with another entry! We canвҖҷt wait to share with all of you!!!:

IвҖҷm the kind of person who requires a posse.

When I decided to go back to school and finish my degree, I had hubby (who is an education

junkie) look at options with me and help nail down transcripts and courses.

When I move houses (which we do often seeing as weвҖҷre a military family) I bribe my best friend

to fly in and help decide my layouts. SheвҖҷs an organizational guru, and I trust her opinions and

insight on what should live where.

I openly admit to crowdsourcing opinions on best restaurants, realtors, and I love hearing

peopleвҖҷs past experiences and thoughts. IвҖҷm a stories person, I feel that hearing other peopleвҖҷs

stories can help better my story.

When it came to surrogacy, I knew I needed a posse. I wasnвҖҷt looking for a company, I was

looking for a circle. I didnвҖҷt need an agency alone, but personal support and insight.

When I first felt the call to become a surrogate, I had reached out to another organization that

wasnвҖҷt CSO. While there was nothing that was a red flag about this company, I knew it wasnвҖҷt

the fit I was looking for. Within my first two interactions with CSO, I knew I had found my posse.

I had access to incredible knowledge, support, and insight from the very beginning. It can

sometimes be a hard knack to balance professionalism with good humour, but itвҖҷs completely

found with CSOвҖҷs staff.

Signed, SecretSurro

Thanks SecretSurro!!! We love your Entries!! CanвҖҷt wait for Part 3вҖҰ Stay Tuned!

records.title: SurroSister Secrets.. Part 7!
records.description: Yay!! Another Entry from SecretSurro to start the weekend!! Tonight is about pie. And personal questions. My intended dad and I share a sweet tooth, and so weвҖҷve determined that IвҖҷll make a pie for dessert for dinner at
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
Yay!! Another Entry from SecretSurro to start the weekend!!

Tonight is about pie. And personal questions.

My intended dad and I share a sweet tooth, and so weвҖҷve determined that IвҖҷll make a pie for dessert for dinner at their house. I love cooking, but this is the first time IвҖҷve actually made a pie вҖ“ desserts arenвҖҷt my strong suit. But I couldnвҖҷt resist the alliteration when I asked my Intended Mom if we could do a вҖңPie and Personal QuestionsвҖқ night, dubbed вҖңP&P.вҖқ

We show up with pie attempt #2. Number one was inedible due to unbearable sweetness, and I heave a big sigh of relief that I had a backup in the pipes. They made chicken parmesan, a fave of ours and fall comfortably into generally chatting. As much as weвҖҷve chatted quite a bit, and weвҖҷre excited about moving forward, I know before we can move to testing we need to get out some nitty gritty. TheyвҖҷll want to know why AM I becoming a surrogate? (I have a fairly considered answer to that, but it still feels like a lot to explain to someone else). My hubby and I wonder how the challenges in their journey have moved them to this point? I still have so much to learn, but I want to know about the technical parts of their (our?) position too. Do they have embryos? How long would we be waiting if they donвҖҷt? And if a transfer fails, do we all want to try again?

Hard questions are hard. Maybe theyвҖҷll be easier with pie.

We finish dinner without having broached any of the necessary personal questions yet. IвҖҷm gearing up to open my mouth when Dad asks, вҖңSo, how did you hear about CSO?вҖқ I let out a nervous laugh, but feel inexplicably jittery getting to the details. I feel emotional recounting all of my whys, and IвҖҷm actually a little shaky in the voice. I didnвҖҷt expect this. There are many moments that I expected big feelings. There are those typical landmark moments that warrant emotions to bubble up. First heartbeat, first positive blood work, gender reveals and announcements. Those moments are when I expected the emotion. But talking about my why, and them talking about how much theyвҖҷve experienced in their journey, and IвҖҷm left shaken.

With every answer, I feel reassurance, and the more openly we talk the more comfortable I feel. These are my people, these are my intended parents, and I canвҖҷt wait to get started with them.

After this slice of pie.

Signed,

SecretSurroвҖҰ

If youвҖҷre reading this post, you are probably considering becoming a surrogate mother, and thatвҖҷs so wonderful. But you likely have many questions as many women do when they start thinking about

how to become a surrogate mother

We wanted to remind you of a few things to think about as you make your decision to become a surrogate. We hope you find this helpful as you start your surrogacy journey, and if there is anything you would like to add about your own decision of becoming a surrogate, please leave us a comment!

First things first вҖ“ always discuss your decision with your

family members and loved ones

. They are the ones who will support you through your journey.

Think about all the wonderful things associated with being a surrogate, and how you are helping another family in need. Our last

blog post

outlined some of these.

If you love being pregnant, then surrogacy is right for you! This one of the biggest factors of becoming a surrogate.

You really must love being pregnant

. Most surrogates are women who are finished growing their families but love being pregnant. ItвҖҷs an amazing thing to do for couple who want a child more than anything, but just cannot grow a family on their own.

Weigh out the options involved. Did you know there are different

types of surrogacy

? ItвҖҷs important to do as much research as possible on surrogacy so you are prepared when you meet with your agency. There are some things you will want to consider, such as who you are comfortable being a surrogate for, and which

type of surrogacy

you feel comfortable with as well.

Consult with a

surrogacy agency

like

Canadian Surrogacy Options

. We will meet with you to discuss everything about surrogacy and present you with thoughts about surrogacy you may not have thought about during your own decision process. We even offer counselling and support groups to ensure that your surrogacy journey is comfortable and you feel supported.

We know that becoming a surrogate is a big decision, even when the idea of it feels natural. But as a

surrogacy agency in Canada

, Canadian Surrogacy Options is committed to the success of your journey and your well-being. We will support you all the way! If you have any questions regarding Canadian surrogacy, please contact us!

## Media Links

records.description: "...We thank you from the bottom of our hearts for your effotrs in making this happen for both of us..." New parents of twins Gestational Surrogacy: Biologically, the Carrier has no genetic link to the child. This can be
records.author: Canadian Surrogacy Options
records.content:
"...We thank you from the

bottom of our hearts

for your effotrs in making

this happen for both of us..."

(New parents of twins)

Gestational Surrogacy:

Biologically, the Carrier has no genetic link to the child. This can be done one of several ways:

Mother's egg, father's sperm

Example: this would be chosen if the biological mother was unable to carry for medical reasons

Donor egg, father's sperm

Example: if there was a question about egg quality of the mother, or there

were no ovaries

Mother's egg, donor sperm

Example: this would be chosen if sperm quality was questioned

Donor egg, donor sperm

Example: this option would be chosen if the "parents" didn't want the Carrier to have any biological link to the child.

Traditional Surrogacy:

The Carrier would be using her own egg, and therefore have a genetic link to the child. In this arrangement, the Surrogate agrees to be inseminated with the sperm of the Intended Father. After the birth, the surrogate agrees to terminate her parental rights, and the Intended Mother does a step-parent adoption.

While this is a very viable and more cost-effective option for many couples, Canadian Surrogacy Options Inc. cannot at this time assist you with this.
